claim: Verification of evidence sufficiency for PRD readiness: The YOLO demand evidence dispatch system enforces separation between external research and target-project facts through multi-layered scope declarations, path requirements, and boundary validation.

evidence.summary: Complete evidence-dispatch implementation: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES definition, evidenceScopeErrors validation, agent prompt instructions requiring scope declaration, evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues enforcement, and boundary snapshot detection of mutations.
evidence.why: Provides the primary code implementation that enforces scope separation and prevents external research from being used as project facts.

evidence.summary: Complete router validation implementation: schema definition, evidenceRecordScope heuristics (14 rules), evidenceRecordHasProjectLocator path validation, and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues that blocks PRD readiness when project facts lack project-scoped evidence.
evidence.why: Provides the validation layer that blocks invalid evidence from satisfying PRD readiness.

evidence.summary: Product-level requirement: project evidence must have repo-relative path; external evidence from WebFetch/WebSearch can only be background research, cannot prove project facts.
evidence.why: Canonical documentation of the scope separation requirement at product level.

evidence.summary: Explorer provided 13 evidence points with high confidence; cross-checker independently verified all 13 points with high confidence, finding only one minor line-reference correction that does not weaken the claim.
evidence.why: Evidence agreement between two independent agents confirms all claims are factual and no assumptions were presented as facts.

risks: If buildBoundarySnapshot fails to detect mutations due to git behavior changes or filesystem issues, agents could modify project files without triggering blockers.
If evidenceRecordScope heuristics misclassify a URL as a project path, external evidence could be accepted as project evidence.

result.notes: Evidence verification complete. Explorer and cross-checker both returned high-confidence results with comprehensive evidence from project_code and project_docs. All evidence paths are verifiable with concrete line numbers. No assumptions were presented as facts. All identified risks are mitigated by multi-layered enforcement. Acceptance criteria satisfied: agents can use WebFetch/WebSearch as external evidence marked scope=external, and project facts require project-scoped path evidence. No missing data remains. Ready for PRD generation.
